title: The Two Paths Of Virtue.
content:
Two are the pathways by which mankind can to virtue mount upward;
    If thou should find the one barred, open the other will lie.
    'Tis by exertion the happy obtain her, the suffering by patience.
    Blest is the man whose kind fate guides him along upon both!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Friedrich Schiller, titled "The Two Paths Of Virtue.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
